info.title: Cloud Workload Protection APIs
info.description:
You can export the OpenAPI specs in the [Cortex Stoplight instance](https://cortex-panw.stoplight.io/docs/cortex-xsiam-3-x/7bi6u9mf8zd6p-cloud-workload-protection-ap-is). 

Cloud Workload Policies and Rules ensure security compliance, prevent misconfigurations, and mitigate risks across cloud environments. Cloud Workload Policies leverage actionable findings or enforce preventive measures at defined stages of the Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC).

 Registry Connectors are used to connect to third-party registries. You can use the Registry Onboarding APIs to onboard third-party registry connectors (such as Docker Hub, GitLab, Harbor, JFrog, and
OpenShift) and managing their instances.

**Required license:** Requires the Cortex Cloud Runtime Security add-on. Not available in Cortex XSIAM Enterprise Plus.

get.description:
Fetches a list of all CWP policy instances. You can filter the results by policy type (e.g., COMPLIANCE, MALWARE, SECRET). By default, all policy types are returned. Use the disableVerbose flag to optimize performance by skipping asset group data updates.

**Required license:** Requires the Cortex Cloud Runtime Security add-on. Not available in Cortex XSIAM Enterprise Plus.

parameters.name: disableVerbose
parameters.description: Flag to bypass calling platform for asset groups data when fetching policies. Note that this means asset groups will not be updated upon fetching policies.

post.description:
Creates new CWP policy. This version supports advanced configuration including policyRules, gracePeriod, and usingSystemAssetGroups.

**Required license:** Requires the Cortex Cloud Runtime Security add-on. Not available in Cortex XSIAM Enterprise Plus.

get.summary: Get a CWP Policy by ID (v2)
get.description:
Fetches the detailed configuration of a single CWP policy identified by its unique ID. This includes associated rules, evaluation stages, and asset scope. Use disableVerbose to exclude platform-specific asset group metadata.

**Required license:** Requires the Cortex Cloud Runtime Security add-on. Not available in Cortex XSIAM Enterprise Plus.

parameters.name: disableVerbose
parameters.description: Flag to bypass calling platform for asset groups data when fetching policies. Note that this means asset groups will not be updated upon fetching policies.

put.summary: Update a CWP Policy by ID (v2)
put.description:
Updates the configuration of an existing CWP policy. The policy ID is mandatory. 
 Note: The policy revision will be automatically incremented upon a successful update. Fields not provided in the request body will be reset to their default values.

**Required license:** Requires the Cortex Cloud Runtime Security add-on. Not available in Cortex XSIAM Enterprise Plus.

requestBody.description: Policy object to update. Policy ID is mandatory. Revision will be auto-incremented. All other data fields in the request will be updated. If a data field is not present, it is considered as filled with the default value and will be updated with the default.

get.description:
Cloud Workload Policies help you prevent and manage security violations in your cloud runtime instances. They enable you to apply detection logic to specific asset groups at the desired SDLC stage, and define what action needs to be taken if the conditions are met.

Get all CWP policies of the given type. Default behavior is all.

**Required license:** Requires the Cortex Cloud Runtime Security add-on. Not available in Cortex XSIAM Enterprise Plus.

post.description:
Add CWP policy instances.

**Required license:** Requires the Cortex Cloud Runtime Security add-on. Not available in Cortex XSIAM Enterprise Plus.

get.summary: Get a CWP Policy by ID (v1)
get.description:
Get a CWP policy by its ID.

**Required license:** Requires the Cortex Cloud Runtime Security add-on. Not available in Cortex XSIAM Enterprise Plus.

delete.summary: Delete a CWP Policy by ID (v1)
delete.description:
Delete a CWP policy by its ID.

**Required license:** Requires the Cortex Cloud Runtime Security add-on. Not available in Cortex XSIAM Enterprise Plus.

parameters.name: closeIssues
parameters.description: If set, will trigger issue closing of issues opened by the deleted policy

put.summary: Update a CWP Policy by ID (v1)
put.description:
Update a CWP policy by its ID.

**Required license:** Requires the Cortex Cloud Runtime Security add-on. Not available in Cortex XSIAM Enterprise Plus.

requestBody.description: policy object to update. Policy ID is mandatory. Revision will be auto-incremented. All other data fields in the request will be updated. If a data field is not present, it is considered as filled with the default value and will be updated with the default.

get.summary: Get the SBOM of the specified asset
get.description:
Get an SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) report for the specified asset. You can specify the format and version of the SBOM, as well as which attribute you would like included.

**Required license:** Cortex Cloud Runtime Security. In Cortex Cloud Posture Management, requires the Cortex Cloud Runtime Security add-on.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The format in which the SBOM is to be exported.

parameters.name: output_format
parameters.description: The version of the SBOM format to be used.

parameters.name: attributes
parameters.description: The list of attributes to be included in the SBOM. If not specified, all fields are included except `Type` and `Name`, which are always included.

parameters.name: assetID
parameters.description: ID of the asset of which you want to export the SBOM

200.description: OK. The request was successful, and the SBOM data is returned in the specified format.

400.description: Bad Request. The request was malformed, missing the required parameters, or the Asset ID provided does not support SBOM Export.

401.description: The API key provided is invalid or missing.

403.description: Forbidden. The API key does not have the necessary permissions to access the requested resource.

404.description: The specified asset was not found.

500.description: An error occurred on the server while processing the request.

201.description: Connector details have been stored successfully.

post.description:
Creates a new registry connector for  based on the provided details.

> **Required license:** 
>
> Cortex Cloud Posture Management or Cortex Cloud Runtime Security.

parameters.name: connectorID
parameters.description: The unique identifier for the connector.

get.description:
Retrieves a registry connector details using its unique ID.

> **Required license:** 
>
> Cortex Cloud Posture Management or Cortex Cloud Runtime Security.

204.description: Connector details have been updated successfully.

put.description:
Updates the attributes of an existing registry connector identified by its unique ID.

> **Required license:** 
>
> Cortex Cloud Posture Management or Cortex Cloud Runtime Security.

delete.description:
Deletes a specific registry connector identified by its unique ID.

> **Required license:** 
>
> Cortex Cloud Posture Management or Cortex Cloud Runtime Security.

tags.name: Policies
tags.description: APIs for managing Cloud Workload Policies

FilterObject.title: Asset Scope Filter
FilterObject.description: Defines the type of assets to be filtered (the scope). You can combine multiple conditions to create complex rules for when the asset filter should be applied.

SEARCH_FIELD.description: Defines the Field to be searched.

SEARCH_TYPE.description: Defines the comparison operator to use for this filter.

SEARCH_VALUE.description: Defines the value to be searched.

VendorSpecificConfig.description: Vendor specific configuration options for scanning.

cluster_id.description: The unique identifier of the OpenShift cluster.

cluster_name.description: The name of the OpenShift cluster.

cluster_asset_id.description: The asset ID associated with the cluster.

cluster_asset_type_id.description: The asset type ID of the cluster.

cluster_realm.description: The realm or environment in which the cluster resides.

repository.description: *Optional*. The Docker Hub repository to scan.

api_domain_name.description: *Optional*. The API domain name to use for the scan.

mode.description:
Specifies the initial scan configuration mode.
 
* `ALL` — Scans all container images, including every tag, in all discovered repositories.
* `LATEST_TAG` — Scans only images tagged as `latest` in all discovered repositories.
* `TAGS_MODIFIED_DAYS` — Scans images in each repository modified within the last *X* days, where *X* is set via an argument. You can select a range of up to 90 days for the scan.

numDays.description: Number of days for the scan mode `lastNDays`

ScanningMode.description: The scanning mode used by the connector

Provider.description: Specifies the cloud provider for the Cortex environment to use for registry scanning.

BrokerType.description: Indicates the Broker VM type used by the Outpost.

enabled.description: Indicates whether the connector is enabled.

region.description: Specifies the region to use for registry scanning. As a best practice, choose the region closest to your registry deployment to achieve the best scanning throughput and potentially reduce cloud costs.

insecure_pull.description: When set to `true`, allows image pulls from insecure registries.

ca_certificate.description: *Optional*. The CA certificate used for authentication.

repository.description: *Optional*. The repository used for image discovery.

allow_static_ips.description: *Optional*. Indicates whether static IP addresses are allowed.

ip_identifier.description: *Optional*. The identifier associated with the IP address.

filter_type.description: Controls whether `asset_scope_filters` act as an inclusion or exclusion filter.

name.description: The name of the registry connector.

enabled.description: Indicates whether the connector is enabled.

auth_token.description: Authentication token used to authorize requests to the API.

insecure_pull.description: When set to `true`, allows image pulls from insecure registries.

ca_certificate.description: *Optional*. The CA certificate used for authentication.

repository.description: *Optional*. The repository used for image discovery.

allow_static_ips.description: *Optional*. Indicates whether static IP addresses are allowed.

ip_identifier.description: *Optional*. The identifier associated with the IP address.

filter_type.description: Controls whether `asset_scope_filters` act as an inclusion or exclusion filter.

Code.description: A short, programmatically safe string indicating the error code reported

EvaluationMode.description: One of the supported evaluation modes

EvaluationStage.description: One of the supported evaluation stages

GetPoliciesResponse.description: The response you get when calling the `get policies` path

PolicyAction.description: One of the supported policy actions

rulesIds.description:
The UUIDs of the rules that define the condition
The `rulesIds` field is only required for non-compliance policies.

condition.description: The `condition` field is only required for non-compliance policies.

PolicySeverity.description: One of the supported policy severities

PolicyType.description: One of the supported policy types

GetPoliciesResponseV2.description: The response you get when calling the `get policies V2` path

PolicyGetResponseDataV2.description: The schema of a returned policy

policyRules.description: Array of policy_rule objects related to the policy

gracePeriod.description: Grace period duration string in days

user_remediation_guidance.description: Additional remediation guidance that can be added to the rule’s predefined guidance.

PolicyPostRequestDataV2.description: The schema of a created policy

policyRules.description: Array of policy_rule objects related to the policy

gracePeriod.description: Grace period duration string in days

PolicyPutRequestDataV2.description: The schema of a created policy

policyRules.description: Array of policy_rule objects related to the policy

gracePeriod.description: Grace period duration string in days
